live_path.role: CLI entrypoint parses --owner and forwards it into the service layer
Service orchestrator for owner resolution, record construction, routing-key derivation, and payload emission
Derives effective owner and owner_source from explicit CLI input or config default
Passes resolved owner into record creation
Builds the persisted base record with name, status, and owner only
Derives routing_key from the already-resolved owner and item name
Creates routing_key from slugified owner and slugified name
Emits final payload by combining stored record with service-derived owner_source and routing_key
Copies the record and appends owner_source and routing_key for emission

owner_source.logic: Returns "explicit" when owner is present and non-blank; otherwise returns "default" with the configured owner.

routing_key.logic: Slugifies owner and name, then joins them with ':'.

test_observations.observation: Explicit owner input yields owner=pm-oncall, owner_source=explicit, and routing_key=pm-oncall:launch-checklist.
Missing --owner falls back to config/defaults.json owner and marks owner_source=default.
CLI forwarding preserves the service-derived owner_source and routing_key; no earlier CLI-side computation is asserted or needed.
Docs are expected to mention --owner, owner_source, routing_key, sync_item, and the configured default owner.

rejected_decoys.reason: Unused by sync_app.service.sync_item; suggests storage-origin owner_source but is not on the live path.
Unused by sync_app.service.sync_item; suggests a storage-adjacent routing path that the live flow does not take.
Unused by sync_app.service.sync_item; reads owner_source from a record, but live records do not carry that field before serialization.
Operational note is contradicted by the live service path and tests.
